From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Roof shape

[Svetitskhoveli Cathedral](/wiki/Svetitskhoveli_Cathedral "Svetitskhoveli Cathedral"), [Mtskheta](/wiki/Mtskheta "Mtskheta"), [Georgia](/wiki/Georgia_(country) "Georgia (country)")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7b/Monasterio_de_Geghard%2C_Armenia%2C_2016-10-02%2C_DD_63.jpg/250px-Monasterio_de_Geghard%2C_Armenia%2C_2016-10-02%2C_DD_63.jpg)](/wiki/File:Monasterio_de_Geghard,_Armenia,_2016-10-02,_DD_63.jpg)

The 13th-century church in the monastery of [Geghard](/wiki/Geghard "Geghard"), Armenia[[1]](#cite_note-1)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/be/Akhtamar_Surb_Khach_Armenian_Church.jpg/250px-Akhtamar_Surb_Khach_Armenian_Church.jpg)](/wiki/File:Akhtamar_Surb_Khach_Armenian_Church.jpg)

The Armenian Apostolic [Cathedral of the Holy Cross](/wiki/Cathedral_of_the_Holy_Cross,_Aghtamar "Cathedral of the Holy Cross, Aghtamar") on [Akdamar Island](/wiki/Akdamar_Island "Akdamar Island"), [Lake Van](/wiki/Lake_Van "Lake Van"), Turkey

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/99/Lalish.jpg/250px-Lalish.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lalish.jpg)

The tomb of [Sheikh Adi](/wiki/Sheikh_Adi_ibn_Musafir "Sheikh Adi ibn Musafir") in [Lalish](/wiki/Lalish "Lalish") (northern Iraq), the religious center of the [Yazidis](/wiki/Yazidis "Yazidis") with holy structures with conical roofs[[2]](#cite_note-2)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a1/Holy_Sepulchre_Church%2C_Cambridge_-_geograph.org.uk_-_466011.jpg/250px-Holy_Sepulchre_Church%2C_Cambridge_-_geograph.org.uk_-_466011.jpg)](/wiki/File:Holy_Sepulchre_Church,_Cambridge_-_geograph.org.uk_-_466011.jpg)

The [Round Church](/wiki/Holy_Sepulchre,_Cambridge "Holy Sepulchre, Cambridge"), Cambridge, England

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/88/Budapestfishermansbastionavertical.jpg/250px-Budapestfishermansbastionavertical.jpg)](/wiki/File:Budapestfishermansbastionavertical.jpg)

[Fisherman's Bastion](/wiki/Fisherman%27s_Bastion "Fisherman's Bastion"), Budapest, Hungary

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/70/Dormition_Church.jpg/250px-Dormition_Church.jpg)](/wiki/File:Dormition_Church.jpg)

[Abbey of the Dormition](/wiki/Abbey_of_the_Dormition "Abbey of the Dormition"), Jerusalem

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/90/Hamdollah1.JPG/120px-Hamdollah1.JPG)](/wiki/File:Hamdollah1.JPG)

[Hamdallah Mustawfi](/wiki/Hamdallah_Mustawfi "Hamdallah Mustawfi") Tomb, [Qazvin](/wiki/Qazvin "Qazvin"), Iran

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f8/Schloss_Neuschwanstein_2013.jpg/330px-Schloss_Neuschwanstein_2013.jpg)](/wiki/File:Schloss_Neuschwanstein_2013.jpg)

[Neuschwanstein Castle](/wiki/Neuschwanstein_Castle "Neuschwanstein Castle"), [Bavaria, Germany](/wiki/Bavaria,_Germany "Bavaria, Germany")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2c/Muenster%2CGermany%2CBuddenturm21.JPG/250px-Muenster%2CGermany%2CBuddenturm21.JPG)](/wiki/File:Muenster,Germany,Buddenturm21.JPG)

Buddenturm ([de](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buddenturm "de:Buddenturm")), a 12th-century defensive tower, in [Münster](/wiki/M%C3%BCnster "Münster"), Germany

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/81/Bundeskunsthalle_Dachgarten.jpg/250px-Bundeskunsthalle_Dachgarten.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bundeskunsthalle_Dachgarten.jpg)

Modern conical roofs on the [Bundeskunsthalle](/wiki/Bundeskunsthalle "Bundeskunsthalle") in [Bonn](/wiki/Bonn "Bonn"), Germany

A **conical roof** or **cone roof** is a [cone](/wiki/Cone "Cone")-shaped [roof](/wiki/Roof "Roof") that is circular at its base and terminates in a point.

## Distribution

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Conical_roof&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Distribution")]

Conical roofs are frequently found on top of towers in [medieval](/wiki/Medieval "Medieval") town [fortifications](/wiki/Fortifications "Fortifications") and [castles](/wiki/Castle "Castle"), where they may either sit directly on the outer wall of the tower (sometimes projecting beyond it to form [eaves](/wiki/Eaves "Eaves")) or form a superstructure above the [fighting platform](/wiki/Fighting_platform "Fighting platform") or terrace of the tower. The latter necessitated the use of spouts to lead the water away over the top of the walls (e.g. as at [Andernach](/wiki/Andernach#Alter_Krahnen "Andernach")'s *Alter Krahnen*). In this case the cone roof was surrounded by a [defensive wall](/wiki/Defensive_wall "Defensive wall"), a [parapet](/wiki/Parapet "Parapet") or a [battlement](/wiki/Battlement "Battlement"). Such conical roofs were usually constructed using a [timber-framed](/wiki/Timber-frame "Timber-frame") support structure covered with [slate](/wiki/Slate "Slate"); more rarely they were made of [masonry](/wiki/Masonry "Masonry").

A small circular [turret](/wiki/Turret_(architecture) "Turret (architecture)") or tourelle with a conical roof is called a pepperpot or pepperbox turret.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Present

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Conical_roof&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Present")]

Today, conical roofs are more often used in rural areas either for circular or small square buildings. They are difficult to construct but use locally available materials.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Conical roofs are widely used in [Armenian](/wiki/Armenian_architecture "Armenian architecture") and [Georgian](/wiki/Architecture_of_Georgia_(country) "Architecture of Georgia (country)") [church architecture](/wiki/Church_architecture "Church architecture").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

A key feature of the [Solomon Islands Parliament Building](/wiki/Solomon_Islands_Parliament_Building "Solomon Islands Parliament Building") is its conical roof.

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Conical_roof&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: See also")]

* [List of roof shapes](/wiki/List_of_roof_shapes "List of roof shapes")
